§6Cores§f

§aFundamental AE2 components for planes and buses.§f

§bCores§f are fundamental crafting components for AE2 machines. There are two types:

§6Formation Core§f (material:43):
§b•§f Used in §bFormation Planes§f
§b•§f Used in §bStorage Buses§f
§b•§f Used in §bImport Buses§f
§b•§f Used in §bME Pattern Providers§f and §bME Interfaces§f (item output side)
§b•§f Performs §a"place" operations§f (inserting items into inventories)

§6Annihilation Core§f (material:44):
§b•§f Used in §bAnnihilation Planes§f
§b•§f Used in §bExport Buses§f
§b•§f Used in §bMolecular Assemblers§f
§b•§f Used in §bME Pattern Providers§f and §bME Interfaces§f (item input side)
§b•§f Performs §c"break" operations§f (extracting items from inventories)

§6Crafting Requirements§f:
Both require §bprocessors§f and §bpure crystals§f. Stockpile these before mass-crafting AE2 machines.

§6Why It Matters§f:
Cores define the §9direction of item flow§f in your network. Every bus and plane that touches an external inventory needs a Core. Plan core production in proportion to your automation scale.
